description: A precious white metal with highest conductivity and reflectivity; the moon's metal. 'Shioli' names what shimmers—the bright metal of reflection and purity.
sound_symbolism: The word shimmers lunar. 'Shi' /ʃi/ gleams with soft shimmer, light catching. The vowel 'o' rounds like full moon. Then 'li' flows with liquid light.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'shioli' (silver). Associated with moon, reflection, purity. Counterpart to 'solai' (gold). For jewelry, mirrors, sacred objects.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Silver's luster—the soft white gleam favored in Art Nouveau jewelry and decoration.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Silver was moon-metal—associated with feminine, night, reflection across cultures.
